title: Drug releasing coatings for medical devices and methods of making same
abstract: This application relates generally to the field of drug releasing insertable devices and methods of making and using same. Specifically, this technology relates to controlled release of drug from a coated balloon directly into affected tissue regions within the body of an individual.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is 1. A drug eluting device, comprising a primer layer containing a water soluble material layer and a multi-part drug layer having a first part and a second part, wherein the first part comprises a macrocyclic triene immunosuppressive compound and the second part comprises at least one polymer-free excipient, wherein polymer-free excipient is a fatty alcohol or fatty aldehyde or a fatty acid or a nonionic surfactant. 2. The medical device according to claim 1 , wherein the device is a drug eluting balloon catheter. 3. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the water soluble material is a globular serum protein having an approximate molecular weight of between 65-70 kD. 4. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the water soluble material comprises a serum protein having at least 90% identity to SEQ ID NO: 1. 5. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the water soluble material is human serum albumin. 6. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the multi-part drug layer is formulated as a single formulation and applied after the primer layer has been applied to a surface of the balloon catheter. 7. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the macrocyclic triene immunosuppressive compound has the following structure: where R is C(0)-(CH 2 ) n -X, n is 0, 1 or 2, X is a cyclic hydrocarbon having 3-8 carbons and optionally contains one or more unsaturated bonds. 8. The device of claim 10, wherein C(0)-(CH 2 ) n -X has one of the following structures: 9. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the macrocyclic triene compound is rapamycin, everolimus, zotarolimus, Biolimus or temsirolimus. 10. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein the multi-part drug layer contains one of tacrolimus, paclitaxel, docetaxel, analogs thereof or mixtures thereof. 1 1. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein polymer-free excipient is a linear fatty alcohol or fatty aldehyde or a fatty acid or a nonionic surfactant containing at least 12 carbon atoms. 12. The device of one of the proceedings claims, wherein polymer-free excipient of the second part of the multi-part drug layer is at least one saturated or unsaturated fatty alcohol and/or at least one saturated or unsaturated fatty aldehyde. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein the secondary excipient is a member selected from the group consisting of heneicosyl alcohol, behenyl alcohol, erucyl alcohol, lignoceryl alcohol, ceryl alcohol, 1 -heptacosanol, montanyl alcohol, 1-nonacosanol, myricyl alcohol, lacceryl alcohol, geddyl alcohol, arachidic acid, behenic acid, lignoceric acid and cerotic acid. 14. A method of manufacturing a drug eluting device, preferably a drug eluting balloon catheter, according to one of the claims 1 till 13 comprising: (a) providing a medical device, preferably a balloon catheter capable of radial expansion once inflated; (b) providing an aqueous solution of a water soluble excipient preferably comprising from about 10% to about 30% of the water soluble excipient in a solution of water; (c) dip coating the device in the solution of (b); (d) allowing the dip coated device to dry; (e) applying a solution comprising a macrocyclic triene immunosuppressive compound and at least one saturated fatty alcohol, polymer- free excipient to the device of (d); and (f) allowing the device of (e) to dry. 15. A medical device according to one of the claims 1 till 14 comprising a balloon catheter having a primer layer comprising a water soluble agent, and a second layer comprising a fatty alcohol lipid excipient and a drug, wherein the water soluble agent is a globular serum protein, the fatty alcohol excipient is a nonionic, linear hydrocarbon and the drug is a lipophilic, macrocyclic triene immunosuppressive compound, further wherein the coating layer is capable of forming cracks after the device is deployed.
